name: ThermalRadiatorSubsystem
description: FMU FAIL: Passes JSON Schema (correct structure, correct fmuReference) and passes all CEL assertions (every individual value is in valid range). However, the combination of emissivity=0.02 and absorptivity=0.85 is pathological: the FMU computes an equilibrium temperature of ~1005 K, which exceeds the physically plausible range (150-400 K) for spacecraft thermal control coatings. The radiator coating would melt long before reaching this temperature.

ownedMember.name: RadiatorPanel
ownedMember.description: A flat-plate spacecraft radiator panel with pathological coating properties.

ownedAttribute.name: emissivity
ownedAttribute.description: Extremely low emissivity — technically valid (0 < ε ≤ 1) but the surface barely radiates

ownedAttribute.name: absorptivity
ownedAttribute.description: High absorptivity — combined with low emissivity, this ratio drives equilibrium temp to ~1005 K

ownedPort.name: thermalInput
ownedPort.description: Incident solar flux and spacecraft internal heat load

ownedAttribute.name: solarIrradiance
ownedAttribute.description: Solar constant at spacecraft orbital distance

ownedMember.name: ThermalCoupling
ownedMember.description: Thermal interface between environment and radiator panel.

ownedMember.name: OperatingTemperatureRange
ownedMember.description: The radiator panel equilibrium temperature shall remain within the allowable range for the thermal coating.
ownedMember.text: The radiator equilibrium temperature shall be between 150 K and 400 K under all operational thermal environments.

ownedMember.name: MassBudget
ownedMember.description: The radiator panel shall not exceed the allocated mass budget.
ownedMember.text: The radiator panel mass (including mounting hardware) shall not exceed 5.0 kg.

ownedMember.description: RadiatorPanel satisfies the operating temperature range requirement.
RadiatorPanel satisfies the mass budget requirement.

ownedMember.name: EquilibriumTemperatureAnalysis
ownedMember.description: Compute radiator equilibrium temperature using Stefan-Boltzmann law. Delegates to the ThermalRadiator FMU for co-simulation.
ownedMember.subject: def-radiator-panel

analysisAction.name: runThermalFMU
analysisAction.description: Execute the ThermalRadiator.fmu with panel properties as inputs.
